title: buxus sempervirens f. prostrata
intro: Buxus sempervirens f. prostrata, also known as the creeping boxwood, is a low-growing, prostrate form of the English boxwood plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Buxaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and its leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of the plant are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, particularly buxine and isobuxine, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the creeping boxwood can be used in teas, infusions, and salves to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
